refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There are no multiple obligations connected by 'and' or 'or' that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to ensure data integrity
Prioritize critical teleoperation data packets to reduce effective packet loss
Establish redundant communication channels to mitigate packet loss
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Interaction between protocol adaptation and teleoperation control loop
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality management by adapting protocol parameters based on network conditions, supporting independence from other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable and timely network condition feedback is available
Risk that adaptation latency may impact teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes customer-defined packet loss limit is achievable under agreed network conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the mechanism for network condition monitoring as a separate DP to further decouple sensing from adaptation logic.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for handling communication protocols and network performance optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to send and receive network performance data and status updates relevant to adaptive communication.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network connection over which adaptive communication protocols operate.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is a core function of network management; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol adaptation; would require significant diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on communication quality but does not control protocol adaptation; moderate diffusion expected.
Safety Services rely on communication but are not responsible for protocol adaptation; high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The adaptive communication protocol logically belongs within the NGHE Solution module, which manages network communication and can best contain the protocol logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design.
Changes in network hardware or external network conditions might require coordination beyond NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive communication protocol to maintain packet loss within limits' directly addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit. The DP logically satisfies the FR by focusing on adaptive protocol implementation to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion (containment score 8) and minimal diffusion (diffusion score 2) for the adaptive communication protocol, indicating good containment of the DP within a single IM. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as this module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for the adaptive protocol implementation.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and minimize diffusion risk.
Include monitoring and feedback mechanisms within the NGHE Solution to verify packet loss compliance in real-time.
Document contingency plans for coordination with external network hardware changes to maintain protocol effectiveness.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a logically sound design parameter and a well-contained implementation module. The adaptive communication protocol DP directly satisfies the FR, and the NGHE Solution IM provides clear ownership with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and recommended revisions pertain to interface definitions and monitoring mechanisms rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and contained design is preferable to avoid complexity and potential errors from deeper decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, include monitoring and feedback mechanisms within the NGHE Solution, and document contingency plans for external network hardware changes, thereby reducing diffusion risks and enhancing verification without further decomposition.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol can be effectively implemented within the NGHE Solution module.
Interface dependencies with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be managed through well-defined contracts.
Packet loss metrics can be monitored in real-time to verify compliance.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules requiring careful interface design.
Need for contingency plans to handle external network hardware changes impacting protocol effectiveness.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss remains within ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms.
Contingency plans for coordination with external network hardware changes.
